hard_constraints.continuity_rule: exact same mechanism state before resume
hard_constraints.base_reference: original faithful chapter-3 12-position sequence remains the backbone
hard_constraints.search_style: theory-guided small branch batch; no random brute force

hypotheses.text: Untested single-gateway mixed-beta diagonal pair: the inner gateway before the late weak block may unlock legal diagonal x±z outer excitation that pure x/z late-anchor searches never touched.
Untested gateway diagonal butterfly: the missing structure may require visiting both legal diagonal outer axes within one closed insertion, not just one diagonal pair.
Mirror-pair control: if the gain is only generic extra excitation, a symmetric early+late diagonal pair should also help; if the gain is truly late-gateway-specific, that control should be weaker.

branch_hypothesis: The missing valid branch is a gateway-diagonal mixed-beta branch: instead of adding more pure x/z late-anchor dose, open the real inner axis at anchor9 to mixed beta, let the outer motor excite diagonal x±z axes legally, and close back exactly before node10 resumes. The strongest form is a diagonal butterfly that visits both legal diagonal outer axes within one continuity-safe insertion.

candidate_specs.rationale: Single legal diagonal pair launched from the anchor9 gateway by opening to beta=-135 and sweeping outer +90/-90. This is the minimal mixed-beta test of whether pure x/z anchor saturation is the real blocker.
Same anchor9 diagonal pair, but reverse the outer sweep order to -90/+90. This tests whether the mixed-beta branch is order-sensitive even before any heavier structural motif is added.

Symmetry control: apply one legal diagonal pair at the early mirror gateway and again at anchor9. If the gain is only from generic extra excitation, this mirror version should help; if the gain is late-gateway-specific, it should not.

Within the same anchor9 gateway, visit both legal diagonal outer axes before returning. This tests whether the missing structure is not a single diagonal, but a two-diagonal butterfly that couples the late block more completely.
Same butterfly idea, but flip the second diagonal sweep sign. This distinguishes “visit both diagonals” from a stricter “same signed sweep on both diagonals” story.
Shorter dwell control for the same-sign butterfly.
Longer-dwell same-sign butterfly. This is the best-mean landed point in the new branch.
Shorter-dwell control for the flip-sign butterfly.
Longer-dwell flip-sign butterfly. This is the best-max landed point in the new branch.

micro_followup_specs.rationale: Post-selection dwell interpolation check above d8.
Post-selection dwell interpolation check above d8.
Post-selection dwell interpolation check above d8.

markov42_rows.rationale: Longer-dwell same-sign butterfly. This is the best-mean landed point in the new branch.
Longer-dwell flip-sign butterfly. This is the best-max landed point in the new branch.

Within the same anchor9 gateway, visit both legal diagonal outer axes before returning. This tests whether the missing structure is not a single diagonal, but a two-diagonal butterfly that couples the late block more completely.
Same butterfly idea, but flip the second diagonal sweep sign. This distinguishes “visit both diagonals” from a stricter “same signed sweep on both diagonals” story.
Shorter dwell control for the same-sign butterfly.
Shorter-dwell control for the flip-sign butterfly.
Same anchor9 diagonal pair, but reverse the outer sweep order to -90/+90. This tests whether the mixed-beta branch is order-sensitive even before any heavier structural motif is added.

Single legal diagonal pair launched from the anchor9 gateway by opening to beta=-135 and sweeping outer +90/-90. This is the minimal mixed-beta test of whether pure x/z anchor saturation is the real blocker.

Symmetry control: apply one legal diagonal pair at the early mirror gateway and again at anchor9. If the gain is only from generic extra excitation, this mirror version should help; if the gain is late-gateway-specific, it should not.

best_new_mean_candidate.rationale: Longer-dwell same-sign butterfly. This is the best-mean landed point in the new branch.

best_new_max_candidate.rationale: Shorter-dwell control for the flip-sign butterfly.

bottom_line.verdict: Yes, a qualitatively new valid direction was found: the anchor9 gateway-diagonal butterfly branch is real and landed at 11.280 / 1.078 / 99.942 (best mean) and 12.909 / 1.780 / 99.859 (best max). But it does not beat the current unified two-anchor winner, and it does not beat the old best legal result on both mean and max simultaneously.

scientific_conclusion: The hidden-branch search succeeded in the structural sense: the search uncovered a previously untested legal family that uses mixed-beta gateway geometry instead of more late10/late11 pure-axis dose. Single diagonal pairs moved the faithful12 path only into the ~13 range, but the butterfly that visits both legal diagonal outer axes inside the same anchor9 insertion collapsed the branch into the low-11 range. The tuned same-sign butterfly reached 11.280 / 1.078 / 99.942, while the tuned flip-sign butterfly reached 12.909 / 1.780 / 99.859. That means the missing branch is real, but the current landed version is still a partial branch: it improves massively over faithful12, slightly beats the old best legal on max in its max-oriented form, yet remains behind the current unified winner and still leaves Ka2_y as the dominant ceiling.
